title: Unexpected behavior when evaluating equality predicates on map keys
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe the unexpected behaviour
The predicate map['key'] = 0 returns different results for map(String, Int16) and map(String, Nullable(Int16)).
It looks like it is treating a missing key as 0 (or, default value) for non-nullable map values, whereas for nullable values it is treated as NULL.
````
CREATE TABLE map_test
(
map_col1 Map(String, Int8),
map_col2 Map(String, Nullable(Int8))
)
ENGINE = Memory;
-- inserting the same data into both columns
INSERT INTO map_test VALUES ({'a':0,'b':0}, {'a':0,'b':0});
INSERT INTO map_test VALUES ({'c':0,'d':0}, {'c':0,'d':0});
INSERT INTO map_test VALUES ({'a':1,'b':0}, {'a':1,'b':0});
-- Returns a row even when key 'a' does not exist
SELECT map_col1
FROM map_test
WHERE map_col1['a'] = 0;
{'a':0,'b':0}
{'c':0,'d':0}  -- key 'a' does not exist
-- Returns the expected result
SELECT map_col2
FROM map_test
WHERE map_col2['a'] = 0;
{'a':0,'b':0}
````
### Which ClickHouse versions are affected?
Latest version
### How to reproduce
https://fiddle.clickhouse.com/0b8eba28-00b4-4267-a137-c719b3243a55
### Expected behavior
Both queries should return same result.
SELECT map_col1 FROM map_test where map_col1['a']=0;
SELECT map_col2 FROM map_test where map_col2['a']=0;
Expected Result:
{'a':0,'b':0}
### Error message and/or stacktrace
_No response_
### Additional context
_No response_
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE map_test_v2 (map_col1 MAP(VARCHAR, INT8), map_col2 MAP(VARCHAR, INT8), map_col3 MAP(VARCHAR, DOUBLE));
INSERT INTO map_test_v2 VALUES (MAP['x', -1, 'y', 1], MAP['x', NULL, 'y', 1], MAP['x', 1.5, 'y', 2.5]);
INSERT INTO map_test_v2 VALUES (MAP['z', 0, 'w', 0], MAP['z', 0, 'w', NULL], MAP['z', 3.14, 'w', 2.71]);
SELECT map_col1 FROM map_test_v2 WHERE map_col1['x'] = -1;
SELECT map_col2 FROM map_test_v2 WHERE map_col2['y'] = 1;
key_bug_features: Different handling of missing keys in map equality predicates
